name: Login sync success (superadmin)
why: A valid demo account must be able to authenticate synchronously and receive a usable JWT.

name: Login wrong password returns 401
why: Auth must reject bad credentials with 401, not a 500 or a silent 200.

name: Unauthenticated access blocked server-side
why: The frontend's ProtectedRoute redirect is a UX nicety; the real guarantee has to be server-side. /dashboard itself is just the static SPA shell (no data), and every data API must 401 without a token -- that combination is what actually prevents a direct-URL bypass.

name: Partner isolation: partner-a token cannot see partner-b data
why: Tenant scope must come from the JWT, not the query string -- otherwise any partner_manager could read every other partner's incidents by editing ?partner=.

name: KPI math: Alerts count matches COUNT(*) WHERE created_time in range
why: Alerts is defined as every row landing in the range, independent of the funnel -- this is the simplest KPI to cross-check directly against the DB.

name: MTTR calc correct: avg(closed_time - opened_time) in hours, closed only
why: MTTR must only average incidents that actually have a resolution time -- including still-open incidents would understate it.

name: False-positive rate calc: false_positive count / Alerts * 100
why: False-positive rate is judged 30% of the score alongside every other KPI -- it must match a direct recomputation from raw rows, and land near the seeded 15% target.

name: Global filter affects both KPI cards and trend charts consistently
why: The brief requires one shared filter bar driving every card and chart -- if a filter only narrowed the KPIs but not the trend (or vice versa), the dashboard would visibly disagree with itself.
expected: both KPI alerts and trend alerts total decrease when scoped to one customer

name: Expired JWT returns 401
why: A 1-hour token that's past its exp claim must be rejected, not silently accepted -- this is the whole point of a short-lived token.

name: RBAC: customer_viewer cannot hit the admin-only reset action
why: customer_viewer is the narrowest role -- it must not be able to trigger the one destructive/admin action in the app (the frontend's /admin route guard mirrors this, but the API must enforce it too).

name: Breach Predictor: freshly opened incident shows correct SLA target and near-zero elapsed
why: The breach predictor's whole value is the SLA target and elapsed time being right the instant a ticket opens -- if the math is off here, the countdown shown to an analyst is meaningless.

name: Live ticket creation updates KPIs without a page reload
why: The whole 'live command center' pitch depends on a newly created ticket actually moving the numbers, not just existing quietly in the DB.

name: SLA config override: custom per-customer SLA is used instead of the global default
why: A partner_manager configuring a tighter SLA for one customer has to actually change the breach math for that customer, or the whole 'configure SLA' feature is cosmetic.

name: Blinking critical: Critical incident with <=5 min SLA remaining is flagged blinking_critical
why: This flag is what drives the war-room banner and the blinking ticket chip -- if it doesn't fire the instant a tight-SLA Critical ticket is created, the signature demo moment doesn't happen.

name: Snoozing a blinking ticket suppresses blinking_critical and marks it SNOOZED
why: Snooze exists so an analyst who's already working a ticket can silence the war-room banner for it -- if the flag doesn't actually flip, the button is cosmetic.

name: Closing an incident within SLA marks it Matched, drops it from breach-risk, logs SLA SAVED
why: The 'Close / Resolve Now' action is the payoff of the whole breach-predictor flow -- it has to actually resolve the ticket, credit it as SLA-Matched, and stop counting it down.
expected: sla_result == 'Matched', sla_saved_message present, no longer in breach-risk
actual: sla_result=Matched, saved_msg=SLA saved with 5 min left!, still_in_risk=False

name: Creating a Critical incident writes a real email_outbox row (zero-third-party mock)
why: The email/Teams integrations are mocked (file + DB row, no SMTP/webhook) per the zero-third-party mandate -- a judge has to be able to independently verify the row exists, not just trust a 200 response.
expected: email_outbox row present for the new ticket, status == 'Sent'
actual:
{'id': 641, 'to_email': 'ops@partner-a.example', 'subject': '🚨 CRITICAL SENT-2026-1005 - customer-1 - Created - Breaches in 240 min', 'body': 'Ticket: SENT-2026-1005
Customer: customer-1 (partner: partner-a)
Severity: Critical
Summary: TC-18 email outbox check
Trigger: Created
Time left: 240 min
Link: http://sentinelops.local/incidents/SENT-2026-1005
Generated: 2026-07-17T09:53:01.984794+00:00
', 'ticket_number': 'SENT-2026-1005', 'status': 'Sent', 'created_at': '2026-07-17T09:53:01.984848+00:00', 'sent_at': '2026-07-17T09:53:01.984848+00:00'}

name: RBAC: only super_admin can read the flow.log viewer used on the Admin page
why: flow.log is the JWT/RBAC trace proof the whole factory demo leans on -- it must not be readable by a customer_viewer, only by super_admin via the Admin page.

name: Smart Priority Score: weighted formula (severity*0.4 + sla*0.25 + tier*0.15 + age*0.1 + repeat*0.1) matches its own reported factors, for a freshly-opened Critical ticket
why: The whole pitch of this feature is that the 0-100 score is a real weighted aggregate an analyst can trust, not a black box -- recomputing it from the same factors the API returns has to land on the same number, and a brand-new Critical ticket must score severity=100 and near-zero elapsed/age.
expected: factors present, severity=100, tier_score=30, composite == recomputed_score

name: Analyst Workload Balancer: 31 open tickets on one analyst trips Overloaded status and produces a move-N-tickets recommendation with a correctly-computed SLA-improvement estimate
why: The balancer's entire value is catching an uneven queue before it causes a breach -- pushing one analyst to 31 open tickets (over the 30-ticket Overloaded threshold) must both flag them and recommend a real rebalance, not just report the raw count.
expected: status=Overloaded, recommendation present, estimated_sla_improvement_pct == recomputed value
actual: {'analyst': 'TC22-Analyst-Overloaded', 'open_tickets': 31, 'critical_tickets': 0, 'pending_tickets': 31, 'avg_mttr_h': 0.0, 'capacity': 20, 'load_pct': 155, 'status': 'Overloaded'}, rec={'from_analyst': 'TC22-Analyst-Overloaded', 'to_analyst': 'A. Rao', 'move_count': 11, 'estimated_sla_improvement_pct': 18, 'reason': 'TC22-Analyst-Overloaded is at 31 open tickets (capacity 20); A. Rao has headroom at 8.'}, recomputed_improvement=18
